<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The footer template still uses Font Awesome icon tags. The project now uses inline SVG icons via `components/icon.html`. This mismatch explains why the social icons aren't rendering.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

The footer still uses Font Awesome `<i>` tags while the theme moved to inline SVG icons — I'll verify `icons.json` and compare the other social-icon templates.

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

The social links were in the HTML, but the icons themselves were invisible.

**Cause:** The site moved from Font Awesome to inline SVGs (`data/icons.json` + `partials/components/icon.html`), and Font Awesome CSS was removed from `hugo.toml`. The footer (and a couple of author templates) still rendered old markup like `<i class="fab fa-facebook"></i>`, which produced empty elements with no icon font behind them.

**Fix:** Those templates now use the icon partial, same as social share and the rest of the site:

```html
{{ partial "components/icon" (dict "Name" .icon) }}
```

Updated files:
- `themes/jorap/layouts/_partials/essentials/footer.html`
- `themes/jorap/layouts/_partials/components/author-card.html`
- `themes/jorap/layouts/authors/single.html`

After a rebuild, the footer outputs proper `<svg>` icons for Facebook, X, GitHub, and LinkedIn, styled with the accent-colored square buttons from `.social-icons`. Refresh the page (or restart `hugo server` if you're running locally) and they should show up.

---

_**User**_

still not seeing the icons

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
SVGs render correctly in the built output. The icon issue likely stems from another source.
